user_query:
I’m preparing a recurring feature on Nigerians thriving abroad, and I want a small, reproducible local data job I can rerun before each publication. Please build a command-line pipeline that reads my profiles from input/stories.jsonl, downloads a trusted country reference to map countries to continents, computes summary stats, and writes a few artifacts I can drop into my drafts.

Requirements:
1) Implement an executable script at scripts/build_diaspora_report.sh (bash as the entrypoint is fine; you may call Python or other CLI tools inside). Running it from the workspace root should:
   - Create data/ and outputs/ (and any needed subfolders).
   - Download the official GeoNames “countryInfo.txt” tab-separated countries reference from the GeoNames project (geonames.org) and save it as data/countryInfo.txt. Do not embed or hardcode a local copy; fetch it at runtime. Use its ISO alpha-2 country code column (often labeled “ISO”) to join with the country_code values in input/stories.jsonl, and its “Continent” column for continent codes (e.g., AF, AS, EU, NA, OC, SA).
   - Parse input/stories.jsonl and compute aggregates: counts per country_code; counts per continent (via the downloaded mapping); counts per sector; and the minimum and maximum award_year.

2) Write the following outputs exactly at these paths:
   - outputs/summary/by_country.csv with columns: country_code,count
   - outputs/summary/by_continent.csv with columns: continent_code,count
   - outputs/summary/by_sector.csv with columns: sector,count
   - outputs/report.md containing, at minimum:
     • Overview: total number of profiles, number of unique countries, and number of continents represented
     • Years covered: MIN_YEAR–MAX_YEAR
     • Top 3 countries by profile count and their counts (if tied, any consistent order is fine)
   - outputs/build.log capturing the start time of the run and one line per produced file with its path and a short note (e.g., row counts where applicable).

3) Make the job re-runnable: it should recreate/overwrite outputs on each run and skip re-downloading data/countryInfo.txt if it already exists (unless a --refresh flag is passed to the script, in which case re-download it).

Assumptions: Avoid manual steps. Do not require credentials. The pipeline must run entirely from this workspace using the provided input and the downloaded GeoNames reference. Exit non-zero on failure so I can trust the run status.

Inputs: input/stories.jsonl
Outputs: data/countryInfo.txt; outputs/summary/by_country.csv; outputs/summary/by_continent.csv; outputs/summary/by_sector.csv; outputs/report.md; outputs/build.log All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
